steps.title: Trace Spanish's role
steps.content: The question asks what point the passage makes about Spanish. In both examples, Spanish sits between an Indigenous source language and English: coyote came from Nahuatl into Spanish and then English, while llama came from Quechua into Spanish and then English. The shared pattern is Spanish as the route into English.

steps.title: Remove unsupported claims
steps.content: Choice A adds Spanish contributions to Indigenous languages, which the passage never says. Choice B turns two examples into a claim about equal numbers of adopted words. Choice D invents a comparison between Spanish and Indigenous contributions to English. Choice C states the actual pattern: Spanish served as a medium for Indigenous influence on English. The answer is C.
